Interface Protocol

public interface Protocol
Text-based wire protocol used by the chat application.

Each command is a single UTF-8 line without line breaks. Client and server exchange commands defined by the constants in this interface. Implementations should treat values as case-sensitive.

Typical flow:
  1. Client connects and sends HANDSHAKE + nick.
  2. Server responds with WELCOME or one of the ERROR constants.
  3. After welcome, messages can be sent using MSG or PRIV.
  4. Server broadcasts messages with FROM and direct messages with PRIV_FROM.
  5. Users list can be requested via LIST_USERS.
  6. Either side closes session by sending QUIT or closing the socket.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final String
    ERR_INVALID_MSG
    Error: message format is invalid.
    static final String
    ERR_INVALID_NICK
    Error: provided nickname is invalid (empty, contains spaces, or exceeds MAX_NICK_LENGTH).
    static final String
    ERR_NICK_TAKEN
    Error: chosen nickname is already reserved by another client.
    static final String
    ERR_NOT_LOGGED_IN
    Error: client attempted an action before completing the handshake.
    static final String
    ERR_USER_NOT_FOUND
    Error: target user for a private message was not found.
    static final String
    ERROR_UNKNOWN
    Error: command was not recognized by the server.
    static final String
    FROM
    Server broadcast line prefix: FROM: <nick> <text>
    static final String
    HANDSHAKE
    Client handshake prefix: HELLO <nick>
    static final String
    LIST_USERS
    Users list command and response prefix: USERS<csv>
    static final int
    MAX_NICK_LENGTH
    Maximum allowed nickname length in characters.
    static final String
    MSG
    Public message prefix: MSG <text>
    static final String
    PRIV
    Direct message prefix: PRIV <nick> <text>
    static final String
    PRIV_FROM
    Direct message server broadcast prefix: PRIV FROM: <from> TO: <to> <text>
    static final String
    PRIV_TO
    Part of PRIV_FROM line separating recipient: TO:
    static final String
    QUIT
    Quit command: QUIT
    static final String
    WELCOME
    Server welcome response confirming successful login: WELCOME
